title: Vapi Municipal election sees 123 polling stations ready: 1.13 lakh voters asked to vote early due to heatwa... - Bhaskar English
full_text:
Vapi Municipal election sees 123 polling stations ready:1.13 lakh voters asked to vote early due to heatwave warning
The general election of Vapi Municipal Corporation will be held on April 26. The election process is in its final stage and all preparations have been finalized by the administration. Special arrangements have been made keeping in mind the scorching summer heat and the possibility of a heatwave.
A total of more than 1.13 lakh voters in the Vapi Municipal Corporation area will exercise their right to vote. 123 polling stations have been prepared for these voters.
According to Election Officer Vimal Patel, primary facilities such as drinking water, shade, and seating arrangements have been checked at all 123 polling stations. These facilities are to ensure that voters do not face any inconvenience in the heat.
Following the heatwave forecast, the help of the health department team has been sought to ensure voters do not face any inconvenience. Keeping in mind the high temperature, special coordination has been established with the health department, so that immediate medical assistance can be provided in any emergency situation.
The election officer has made a special appeal to voters to cast their votes early in the morning. Voting will take place from 7:00 AM to 6:00 PM. To avoid the afternoon heat and heatstroke, it is advisable for voters to cast their votes as much as possible before 10:00 AM. Voting early in the morning will provide voters relief from both long queues and the heat.
Enthusiasm is visible among voters in Vapi regarding the elections. The administration has geared up to ensure peaceful and well-equipped voting.
